rulings.Case Title: KEN ELDER V. J.P. MORGAN CHASE N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Approve Settlement And Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 11, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF KEN ELDER's Motion To Approve Settlement And Dismissal. Relator Ken elder's unopposed Motion To Approve Settlement And Dismissal is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and the settlement appearing to be in the best interests of the parties involved and consistent with the purposes behind the CFCA under Government Code section 12652(c)(1), the court approves the settlement of this action as set out in Exhibit 2 of the Golub Declaration, dated October 28, 2025, and, pursuant to the settlement, the court orders dismissal of this action with prejudice as to the claims of both Relator and the State. Under Government Code section 12652(g)(3), the court approves an award of a relator's fee in the amount of $8,375.00 and orders same. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CARNEICE KATHERINE HALL-JOHNSON VS. CITIBANK N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Carniece Kathrine Hall-Johnson Iso Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 11, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF CARNEICE HALL-JOHNSON's Motion For Relief From Stay. Plaintiff Carneice Kathrine Hall-Johnson's Motion For Relief From Stay is GRANTED. Good cause appearing the stay established by stipulation and Order filed June 13, 2024, is lifted. The case is retuned to the active civil case list/master calendar. Defendant Citibank, N.A.'s response to the complaint is due within 28 days of this order. Defendant's argument that the case should be dismissed as duplicative is not properly before the court at this time. Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. PETRAGEOUS DESIGNS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 11, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff Gabriel Espinoza's unopposed Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and, (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the Parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address =(302/JMQ)
